CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Continued To Oct-21-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ACE ROOFING SF, INC., YONG CHAN CHON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants Ace Roofing SF, Inc. and Yong Chan Chon's demurrer to the complaint is overruled. There is no requirement that a plaintiff suing for breach of contract attach the written contract on which it is suing; rather, " ' a plaintiff may plead the legal effect of the contract rather than its precise language.'" (Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 394, 402 [holding that "plaintiff's failure either to attach or to set out verbatim the terms of the contract was not fatal to his breach of contract cause of action"].) Paragraph 7 of the complaint adequately pleads the legal effect of the parties' contract. Defendants' argument that the complaint is barred by the two-year statute of limitations applicable to oral contracts fails because, as Defendants concede, the Complaint explicitly alleges that it is based on a "written agreement." (E.g., Compl. pars. 16-17.) Defendants' demurrer on the ground that Defendant Chon cannot be found personally liable is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant Chon was a party to the contract. (Compl. par. 7.) Defendants' reliance on the purported terms of the written proposal and Plaintiff's discussion of the contents of exhibits attached to its opposition are improper, as a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or properly subject to judicial notice.

Plaintiff's opposition to the demurrer was late-filed. Plaintiff is admonished that in the future, late filings may not be considered by the Court.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
